facts.text: Gowanda earned the nickname "America's glue capital" after a 1976 book declared the Eastern Tanners Glue Company the world’s largest glue plant.
The New York & Lake Erie Railroad depot in Gowanda still runs freight and occasional passenger trains, a living reminder of the town’s rail heritage.
The historic Hollywood Theatre, a restored 1920s venue, hosts the annual Hollywood Happening festival, spotlighting local music and arts.
The Peter Cooper Corporation Glue Factory and Moench Tannery once polluted Cattaraugus Creek, illustrating Gowanda’s industrial past.
The 1987 film "Planes, Trains and Automobiles" filmed scenes at Gowanda Train Depot and Olympia Diner, linking the town to Hollywood.
